content: The Recruitment Rules for Non-Teaching staff in NITs were initially issued on 20 December 2017 following approval by the Council of NITSER.
The Oversight Committee was constituted under Prof. Sivaji Chakravorti to address anomalies, stagnation, and career progression issues in the Non-Teaching Recruitment Rules.
The Oversight Committee submitted its report on 4 September 2018 and further discussed inconsistencies in a meeting held on 19 January 2019.
The Ministry approved the recommendations of the Oversight Committee and issued revised Non-Teaching Recruitment Rules (2019) replacing the Recruitment Rules of 2017.

Ad-hoc, temporary, contractual, and outsourced employees of RECs and NITs may receive one-time age relaxation to participate in the first regular recruitment process, subject to Board of Governors approval.
Employees granted higher Grade Pay under MACP may be mapped to positions corresponding to their Grade Pay, subject to vacancy availability within the same cadre.
Six cadres are identified for mapping: Support Cadre, Lower Ministerial, Higher Ministerial, Lower Technical, Higher Technical, and Pharmacist Cadre.
Mapped employees may be considered for promotion up to one stage above the feeder cadre or two stages above the next below feeder cadre, subject to qualifying service requirements.
REC-era officers not previously mapped to Group-A officer cadres may be mapped to the lowest officer cadre position as a one-time measure.
Each non-teaching cadre, except the Higher Stenographer cadre, shall have four designations distributed in the ratio 4:3:2:1.
For cadres with fewer than four positions, at least one position shall be reserved for promotion.
Regular NIT staff appointed before implementation of Recruitment Rules may participate in selection processes irrespective of age and percentage of marks requirements.
Registrars completing a five-year term may apply for appointment to another term if they satisfy educational qualification and experience requirements.
Recruitment of Hindi staff in NITs and IIEST shall follow the Recruitment Rules prescribed by the Department of Official Languages.
The post of Security Officer remains abolished under the Recruitment Rules 2017, though existing officers may continue and be retrained for alternative responsibilities.
No relaxation in educational qualification requirements has been recommended by the Oversight Committee.
Institute-specific issues shall be resolved by the respective Institutes and Boards in accordance with NITSER Act provisions, statutes, and government instructions.
The revised Non-Teaching Recruitment Rules (2019) replace the Recruitment Rules issued on 20 December 2017.
All Institutes shall adopt and strictly implement the Non-Teaching Recruitment Rules (2019) after approval by their Boards of Governors.
